title: Height Cumulative
subject: Mathematics
objective:
To determine experimental probability
of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6 by throwing a die
500 times and compare them with their
theoretical probabilities.
A fair die, pen, sheets of white
paper.
procedure:
1. Divide the students of the class into ten groups I, II, III, IV, V, VI, VII, VIII,
IX and X of suitable size.
2. Each group will throw a die 50 times and will observe the occurrence of
each 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 6.
3. Count the total number of times (frequency) 1 comes up in each group and
denote it by   a 1 ,   a 2 ,   a 3   ,....,   a 10 , respectively.
4. Calculate the experimental probability of 1 appearing in each group as
3   10 1   2
,   ,   , ......,
50   50   50   50
a   a a   a
5. Calculate the experimental probability of 1 based on the data of 1st group,
1st two groups, ....., all ten groups as
1   2   3   1   2   10 1   1   2   ...
,   ,   , ...,
50   100   150   500
a   a   a   a   a   a a   a   a   +   +   +   +   + +   , respectively.
6. Similarly, calculate the expermental probability of 2 based on the data of
1st group, 1st two groups, ......., all the ten groups as   1
50
b   ,   1   2
100
+ b   b   ,   1   2   3
150
+   + b   b   b   ,
...,   1   2   10 ...
500
+   +   + b   b   b   , respectively.
7. Proceed in the same way for getting experimental probabilities for 3, 4, 5 and 6.
discussion:
On the same squared paper, if for a given frequency distribution, “less than type
ogive” and “more than type ogive” are drawn, then the   x -coordinate of the point
of intersection of these ogives will give the “median” of the data.
174   Laboratory Manual
